permissions._comment: Repo-shared allowlist for the VM-side claude-vm-runner. Scoped to read-only diagnostic commands + the two narrow self-heal restart paths (ict-web-api.service via vm-web-api-recover.yml, ict-trader-live.service via system-actions.yml::restart-bot-service). Per docs/claude/system-actions.md and docs/claude/diag-relay.md, restarting ict-web-api has no trading blast radius (read-only API). Adding entries: mirror the audit pattern in scripts/ops/*.sh — narrow as possible.

hooks._comment: Project-level skill announcement. Forces every web-session to know the THREE review skills exist and what each one's scope is (split 2026-05-26), so we never repeat the 'Claude treats /health-review as a code-quality audit' confusion (operator feedback 2026-05-10) AND so the new /performance-review and /ml-review are not silently ignored. The hook simply prints a directive to stdout — Claude Code on the web injects stdout into the session-init context, so Claude reads it before the first user prompt. The cat fallback ('|| true') keeps the hook from failing the session if the skill file is ever missing.
hooks.command: echo '=== PROJECT-LEVEL SKILLS: three-way review split (2026-05-26) ==='; echo ''; echo '/health-review — TECHNICAL/PIPELINE/DATA health. Reads .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md. Output: comms/schema/health_review_response.template.json. Backlog: docs/claude/health-review-backlog.json.'; echo ''; echo '/performance-review — TRADING + STRATEGY performance + per-decision A-F grading. Reads .claude/skills/performance-review/SKILL.md. Output: comms/schema/performance_review_response.template.json. Backlog: docs/claude/performance-review-backlog.json. Owns comms/claude_strategy_scores.jsonl.'; echo ''; echo '/ml-review — MODEL + TRAINING lifecycle, promotion/demotion, experiments. Reads .claude/skills/ml-review/SKILL.md. Output: comms/schema/ml_review_response.template.json. Backlog: docs/claude/ml-review-backlog.json.'; echo ''; echo 'For ALL three: Claude pulls the LIVE BOT runtime state itself via the diag relays — NEVER ask the operator to paste a snapshot. Each posts a one-line update to @claude_ict_comms_bot via the send-ping system-action. NONE are code reviews or codebase audits — do NOT fall back to the review or security-review skills, and do NOT ask scoping questions (the scope of each is fixed in its SKILL.md).'; echo ''; echo 'Skill files:'; ls -la .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md .claude/skills/performance-review/SKILL.md .claude/skills/ml-review/SKILL.md 2>/dev/null || echo '(one or more missing — should never happen on main)'

log="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/logs/notify_hook.log"; mkdir -p "$(dirname "$log")"; cp_line=$(grep -m1 '^## CP-' "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/claude/checkpoints/CHECKPOINT_LOG.md" 2>>"$log" || echo ''); [ -z "$cp_line" ] && { echo "$(date -Iseconds) skip: no CP entry found" >>"$log"; exit 0; }; cp_id=$(echo "$cp_line" | grep -oE 'CP-[0-9-]+' | head -1); title=$(echo "$cp_line" | sed 's/^## CP-[0-9-]* — //'); cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && { echo "$(date -Iseconds) firing $cp_id" >>"$log"; PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/notify_session.py session --checkpoint "$cp_id" --summary "$title" >>"$log" 2>&1; echo "$(date -Iseconds) exit=$?" >>"$log"; } || true
